verses.1: Woe to you who destroy, but you weren’t destroyed,
verses.2: LORD, be gracious to us. We have waited for you.
verses.3: At the noise of the thunder, the peoples have fled.
verses.4: Your plunder will be gathered as the caterpillar gathers.
verses.5: The LORD is exalted, for he dwells on high.
verses.6: There will be stability in your times, abundance of salvation, wisdom, and knowledge.
verses.7: Behold, their valiant ones cry outside;
verses.10: “Now I will arise,” says the LORD.
verses.12: The peoples will be like the burning of lime,
verses.13: Hear, you who are far off, what I have done;
verses.14: The sinners in Zion are afraid.
verses.17: Your eyes will see the king in his beauty.
verses.18: Your heart will meditate on the terror.
verses.19: You will no longer see the fierce people,
verses.20: Look at Zion, the city of our appointed festivals.
verses.21: But there the LORD will be with us in majesty,
verses.22: For the LORD is our judge.
verses.24: The inhabitant won’t say, “I am sick.”
